# Project Guidelines

## Stack

- **Nuxt 4.3** with `app/` directory layout (Nuxt 4 convention — source files live in `app/`, not root)
- **@nuxt/content v3** with `better-sqlite3` as local content database
- **Vue 3** Composition API (`<script setup lang="ts">` everywhere)
- **TypeScript** — configs generated by Nuxt into `.nuxt/`; do not edit `tsconfig.json` directly

## Build & Dev Commands

```bash
npm run dev        # start dev server at http://localhost:3000
npm run build      # production build
npm run generate   # static site generation
npm run preview    # preview production build
npm run lint       # lint with ESLint + Prettier
npm run lint:fix   # auto-fix lint/format issues
```

## Architecture

### Content System

- Content files live in `content/` (Markdown, MDC syntax supported)
- Collection defined in `content.config.ts` — a single `content` collection with `type: 'page'`
- Query pattern: `queryCollection('content').path(route.path).first()`
- Rendering: `<ContentRenderer :value="page" />`
- 404: throw `createError({ statusCode: 404, fatal: true })` when `page` is null

### Routing

- `app/pages/[...slug].vue` — catch-all for all content pages
- `app/pages/index.vue` — custom home page (currently a shell; does not query content)

### MDC Components

Vue components in `app/components/` are auto-registered and usable in Markdown via MDC syntax:

```md
::alert{color="green"}
Content goes here
::

::counter
::
```

- `Alert.vue` — accepts `color` prop (String, default `'orange'`), uses `<slot>`
- `Counter.vue` — no props, no slot; purely interactive (increment/decrement)

## Code Style

- **ESLint + Prettier** — `prettier/prettier` violations are ESLint errors
- Run `npm run lint:fix` before committing
- Flat Config format via `withNuxt()` in `eslint.config.mjs`
- Stylistic rules enabled via `@nuxt/eslint` module
